mentions.note: Good quarter with 6.5% yield unable to rally; stagflation fears too powerful to buck despite improving fundamentals.
Food company considerably lower; inflation signal affecting packaged foods.
At multi-year lows. 6.67% yield does not stop decline. Comparable Smucker reported well but got crushed immediately. Consumer staples facing structural headwinds. No reason to recommend; pass.
Food group struggling; needs M&A to reaccelerate organic growth; pricing and pet food alone insufficient; core categories flat/declining.
One of most reliable stocks in market now hitting new lows with 7.2% yield; market hasn't caught into belief of dividend benefits despite high yield.
Blowout quarter and $3B cost-cut announcement sparked 8.5% rally and food-stock following, but structural headwinds (GOP-1, rising costs, shallow growth) persist; gains ephemeral.
